schema.appDescription: Calculate maximum loan amount you are eligible for based on net income, existing EMIs, and lender FOIR norms. Applies for home, car, and personal loans.

inputs.label: Existing Monthly EMIs (₹)
inputs.hint: Total of all current loan EMIs

outputs.label: Current FOIR (Fixed Obligation to Income Ratio)

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the Loan Eligibility Calculator. All inputs have smart defaults.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time as you type — no submit button needed.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown to see how numbers evolve year by year.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust inputs to compare different scenarios and find the best option for you.

faq.q: How do banks calculate loan eligibility?
faq.a: Banks use FOIR (Fixed Obligation to Income Ratio) — typically 40–50% of net income. If your net salary is ₹80,000, you can have total EMIs (existing + new) of ₹32,000–₹40,000. The remaining EMI capacity determines the maximum loan. Credit score, employer category, and age also affect final eligibility.

faq.q: What FOIR do banks use for home loans?
faq.a: Most banks allow FOIR up to 50% for home loans with good credit scores. SBI uses 50% FOIR, HDFC 50%, ICICI up to 55% for high-income applicants. For personal loans, FOIR is typically capped at 40–45%.

faq.q: How can I increase my loan eligibility?
faq.a: To increase eligibility: clear existing loans to reduce FOIR, add co-borrower (spouse), improve CIBIL score above 750, increase tenure (reduces EMI per lakh), choose a lender offering higher FOIR, and demonstrate stable income with ITR + salary slips.

faq.q: Is the Loan Eligibility Calculator free?
faq.a: Yes, completely free with no registration on CalcPhi.
No. All calculations run in your browser. We never store your financial data.

faq.q: Can I use the Loan Eligibility Calculator on mobile?
faq.a: Yes. CalcPhi works on all modern smartphones and tablets.

examples.heading: ₹80,000 net salary — home loan eligibility
examples.text: Net income ₹80,000, existing EMI ₹10,000. Available EMI capacity at 50% FOIR = ₹40,000 − ₹10,000 = ₹30,000. At 8.5% for 20 years, max home loan = approximately ₹28.6 lakh.

examples.heading: Adding co-borrower to boost eligibility
examples.text: Adding a co-borrower with ₹60,000 net income raises combined income to ₹1.4 lakh. At 50% FOIR, combined EMI capacity = ₹70,000. This doubles the eligible loan amount to approximately ₹60 lakh.
